title: Control test freshness — evidence older than policy cadence
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate whether evidence content is sufficient; focuses on freshness.
evidence: Weekly 'control_test_freshness' writes overdue-evidence findings to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC5.1 establishment of control activities — freshness is required for continuous operation.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC5.1 (Control activities) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.47.1: Control test freshness — evidence older than policy cadence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.47.1' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.36 compliance with information security policies — evidence freshness is the operational control.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When any in-scope control has no test-of-operation evidence within its policy cadence, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Controls with current evidence do NOT fire.

spl:
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup control_test_log.csv control_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| table control_id control_family cadence_days last_tested age_days
description: Cross-references the control inventory with the evidence ledger. Produces a list of controls whose evidence is older than their policy cadence.
value: Provides internal audit with a continuously-updated testing backlog rather than a pre-audit scramble.
implementation: (1) Maintain control_inventory.csv with cadence per control; (2) emit control_test_log.csv from GRC tooling; (3) run weekly.
visualization: Heatmap of control × age, table of overdue, single-value '% of controls with fresh evidence'. Key fields: `age_days`, `cadence_days`, `control_family`, `control_id`, `last_tested`.
knownFalsePositives: Controls mid-transition (ownership change) may legitimately exceed cadence; tag with a transition flag.
grandmaExplanation: We check on cross-references the control inventory with the evidence ledger. produces a list of controls whose evidence is older than their policy cadence. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain control_inventory.csv with cadence per control; (2) emit control_test_log.csv from GRC tooling; (3) run weekly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup control_test_log.csv control_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| table control_id control_family cadence_days last_tested age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Control test freshness — evidence older than policy cadence** — Provides internal audit with a continuously-updated testing backlog rather than a pre-audit scramble.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>cadence_days` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: age_days, cadence_days, control_family, control_id, last_tested.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of control × age, table of overdue, single-value '% of controls with fresh evidence'.
